comment: Covers indexing retrieval and generation with supporting LangChain chunks.
Names and explains expected RAGAS metrics but citations mostly support metric names rather than full definitions.
Explains LangGraph stateful workflow support and its relation to LangChain agents.
Explains FAISS similarity search and its role as vector retrieval infrastructure for RAG.
Correctly identifies Self-RAG and explains reflection tokens and adaptive retrieval.
Covers reflection planning tool use multi-agent collaboration and dynamic retrieval workflow.
Provides several relevant projects but misses Dify and CrewAI from the expected project set.
Correctly avoids an absolute winner but only partially compares the expected methods and gives weak CRAG coverage.
Forms a useful LangChain versus LlamaIndex design comparison but some cited chunks are broad or only partially supportive.
Clearly distinguishes Self-RAG internal reflection from CRAG external retrieval evaluation and correction.
Correctly contrasts Dify as a broader LLM app platform and RAGFlow as a RAG-focused engine.
Only describes one survey classification and refuses the SoK comparison although the task is answerable from references.
Refuses to classify the paper list and does not produce the required type distribution.
Lists several broad RAG improvement directions but misses key expected methods such as Self-RAG A-RAG TeaRAG and KG-RAG.
Correctly classifies the four projects but citations are only partially aligned with the expected evidence chunks.
Computes both averages and the 0.1025 difference correctly from the metric table.
Identifies arXiv API and Fetcher but misses urllib direct request as the third data acquisition method.
Correctly identifies save_md parameters and the saved metadata fields.
Correctly calculates the largest C0 to C2 metric improvement and cites the metric table.
Correctly states evidence is insufficient for an all-task superiority claim but citations only partially support the boundary reasoning.
